summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Runtime Module Rework (Planned Runtime Model). All 60 backend tests passed (100% success rate). All critical frontend UI tests passed. The new planned-runtime model is fully functional: manual input is Planned Runtime hours per line per date, downtime is auto-derived from breakdowns (warnings excluded), availability is calculated as (Planned - Downtime) / Planned × 100 with clamping at 0%, and unlogged days are properly marked. Control Room and Analytics pages load without errors and display runtime data correctly.

backend_issues.notes: All backend APIs working correctly. No issues found.

passed_tests: Backend: Admin login (admin/admin123) - token received
Backend: Tech login (tech/tech123) - token received
Backend: Reject planned_hours > 24 (400 error)
Backend: Reject planned_hours = 0 (400 error)
Backend: Reject negative planned_hours (400 error)
Backend: Reject legacy body format with calendar_hours/run_hours (422 error)
Backend: Create planned runtime 16h - response includes planned_hours, downtime_hours, run_hours, availability, clamped flag
Backend: Initial availability is 100% when no breakdowns exist
Backend: Create planned runtime 8h
Backend: Create and close 2h breakdown - downtime is exactly 120 minutes
Backend: RCA triggered for breakdown > 30min (rca_required=true, rca_task_id returned)
Backend: Derived downtime ≈ 2h from breakdown records
Backend: Derived run hours ≈ 14h (16h planned - 2h downtime)
Backend: Availability ≈ 87.5% ((16-2)/16 × 100)
Backend: Clamped flag is false when downtime < planned
Backend: Create planned runtime 1h with 2h downtime - availability clamped to 0%
Backend: Clamped flag is true when downtime > planned
Backend: Warning created successfully (POST /api/warnings with machine_id, description, warning_type)
Backend: Downtime unchanged after warning creation (warnings excluded from downtime)
Backend: Control Room custom date range (GET /api/control-room/line-kpis?date_from&date_to) - returns lines array with planned_minutes, downtime_minutes, availability, clamped field
Backend: PC21 line has all required fields in Control Room response
Backend: Control Room live 24h window (GET /api/control-room/line-kpis?hours=24) - all lines have non-null availability (24/7 fallback for unlogged days)
Backend: Analytics plant level (GET /api/analytics/kpis?level=plant) - has planned_hours, run_hours, availability, availability_trend
Backend: Analytics response does NOT have calendar_hours key (legacy removed)
Backend: Analytics machine level inherits line's planned hours
Backend: Machine detail endpoint (GET /api/machines/{id}) - runtime block has planned_hours, downtime_hours, run_hours, logged_days, availability
Backend: CSV import with planned model (line,date,planned_hours) - imported successfully
Backend: CSV rejects legacy columns (run_hours) with 400 error
Backend: Delete runtime entry (DELETE /api/line-runtime-logs?line&date) - returns 200
Backend: Day reverts to unlogged after deletion
Backend: Reliability metrics endpoint (GET /api/reliability/metrics) - returns 200 with machine metrics including mtbf
Frontend: Login successful with admin/admin123
Frontend: Runtime page navigation working
Frontend: Page title correct: 'Runtime Tracking — Planned Runtime Per Line'
Frontend: Planned Runtime KPI card found (data-testid contains runtime-kpi-planned or kpi-planned)
Frontend: Downtime KPI card found (data-testid contains runtime-kpi-downtime or kpi-downtime)
Frontend: Calendar view displays 31 day cells
Frontend: 12 'unlogged' labels visible in calendar for days without data
Frontend: Day dialog opens when clicking a day cell
Frontend: Per-line rows found in day dialog (PC21 visible)
Frontend: Planned hours input found in day dialog (data-testid=day-planned-{line})
Frontend: Derived fields visible in day dialog (downtime, run, availability with data-testids day-downtime-{line}, day-run-{line}, day-avail-{line})
Frontend: Entry dialog opens with 'Add Entry' or 'Log Planned Runtime' button
Frontend: Entry dialog has Planned Runtime input (data-testid=runtime-planned-input)
Frontend: NO legacy calendar_hours input in entry dialog
Frontend: NO legacy run_hours input in entry dialog
Frontend: Table view toggle button found (data-testid=runtime-view-toggle)
Frontend: Table view displays after toggle with correct columns: Date, Line, Department, Machines, Planned h, Downtime h (derived), Run h, Availability, Source
Frontend: Table shows 2 data rows with correct values (16h/2h/14h/87.5% and 20h/0h/20h/100%)
Frontend: Control Room page loads without errors
Frontend: Control Room displays line cards with availability/downtime KPIs
Frontend: Analytics page loads without errors
Frontend: Analytics page displays charts/visualizations
Frontend: No JavaScript errors in Control Room
Frontend: No JavaScript errors in Analytics

backend_planned_runtime_creation.create_16h_entry: ✅ PASS - Response includes all derived fields (planned_hours=16.0, downtime_hours=0.0, run_hours=16.0, availability=100.0, clamped=false)

backend_control_room_apis.custom_date_range: ✅ PASS - Returns lines array with planned_minutes, downtime_minutes, availability, clamped
backend_control_room_apis.live_24h_window: ✅ PASS - All lines have non-null availability (24/7 fallback)

backend_analytics_apis.plant_level: ✅ PASS - Has planned_hours, run_hours, availability, availability_trend. NO calendar_hours key.
backend_analytics_apis.machine_level: ✅ PASS - Machine inherits line's planned hours

backend_machine_detail.runtime_block: ✅ PASS - Runtime: planned=61.0h, down=6.0h, run=56.0h, days=5, availability present

backend_delete.verify_unlogged: ✅ PASS - Day reverted to unlogged (0 items)

backend_reliability_regression.metrics_endpoint: ✅ PASS - Returns list with machine_id, mtbf fields

frontend_runtime_page.page_title: ✅ PASS - 'Runtime Tracking — Planned Runtime Per Line'
frontend_runtime_page.kpi_cards: ✅ PASS - Planned Runtime and Downtime KPI cards found
frontend_runtime_page.day_dialog: ✅ PASS - Opens, shows per-line rows with planned input and derived fields
frontend_runtime_page.entry_dialog: ✅ PASS - Has Planned Runtime input, NO legacy calendar_hours/run_hours
frontend_runtime_page.table_view: ✅ PASS - Toggle works, displays table with correct columns (Date, Line, Planned h, Downtime h (derived), Run h, Availability, Source)

frontend_regression.control_room: ✅ PASS - Loads without errors, displays line cards with KPIs
frontend_regression.analytics: ✅ PASS - Loads without errors, displays charts

planned_runtime_model.manual_input: ✅ VERIFIED - Only Planned Runtime hours per line per date (0 < h <= 24)
planned_runtime_model.downtime_derivation: ✅ VERIFIED - Auto-derived from breakdown records, union-merged, open breakdowns tick live
planned_runtime_model.warnings_exclusion: ✅ VERIFIED - Warnings do NOT count as downtime
planned_runtime_model.clamped_flag: ✅ VERIFIED - Data-quality flag when downtime > planned
planned_runtime_model.24_7_fallback: ✅ VERIFIED - Live windows use 24h assumption for unlogged days
planned_runtime_model.single_source_of_truth: ✅ VERIFIED - Control Room, Analytics, machine detail all use kpi_engine.py
planned_runtime_model.legacy_removal: ✅ VERIFIED - calendar_hours/run_hours inputs removed, CSV rejects legacy format

notes: All test data was cleaned up after testing (breakdowns, warnings, runtime entries deleted)
RCA auto-creation for breakdowns >30min is working as expected (not a bug)
The machine detail endpoint is /api/machines/{id} (not /summary or /detail)
Table view initially appeared not to work due to insufficient wait time in first test, but detailed testing confirmed it works perfectly
Screenshots captured show correct UI rendering with cyberpunk aesthetic maintained
